Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search or list Airtable Stipend Transactions records for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: Stipend Transactions, field: Account, value: "Mosaic Robotics"). Find Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021, Approval Status: Approved, Review Notes state it was auto-approved by ExpenseBot in error because the $800 furniture cap was stored as a string), Andre Washington's $350 coworking space membership (recStip000000022, Approval Status: Approved, auto-approved in error because excluded_categories was stored as string not array), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes (recStip000000023, Approval Status: Approved, same excluded_categories config bug). All three still show "Approved" despite being identified as erroneous during the pilot audit. Also find Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Approval Status: Pending Approval, flagged by Dmitri with a duplicate SHA-256 hash matching Kevin Tran's submission).
OE 2: Search or list Airtable Stipend Transactions records for Vectral Systems using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: Stipend Transactions, field: Account, value: "Vectral Systems"). Find Kevin Tran's $89 ergonomic keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, Approval Status: Approved, flagged by Dmitri's audit as a duplicate hash match with Andre Washington's identical submission). Also find records that were false rejections from the ExpenseBot stale defaults: Maria Santos's $550 standing desk (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, Approval Status: Pending Approval, never manually corrected), James Okafor's $82 ergonomic keyboard tray (recStip000000019, Approval Status: Rejected, double bug - miscategorized as office_supplies instead of ergonomic AND used stale $50 default limit, never manually corrected), and Lisa Park's $130 WiFi system (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi, Approval Status: Pending Approval, never manually corrected). These three are the uncorrected Vectral false rejections. The other two - Danielle Osei's $475 chair and Kevin Tran's $185 internet - were manually overridden and approved by Mina.
OE 3: List ExpenseBot-related Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or by filtering on the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project (proj_expense_autocategorizer). Find the full set of tickets: pilot monitoring (ENG-211, in_progress, P1), Vectral config fix (ENG-214, in_progress, P1, assigned to Lena), Mosaic config fix (ENG-215, in_progress, P1, assigned to Lena), full audit of 47 pilot expenses (ENG-216, in_progress, Urgent, assigned to Dmitri), pipeline hardening (ENG-217, in_progress, P1, assigned to Lena), dashboard type validation (ENG-218, in_progress, P2, assigned to Anh), launch readiness checklist (ENG-219, to_do, P2, assigned to Samira), and stipend duplicate detection (ENG-220, to_do, P1, assigned to Dmitri).
OE 4: Get full details on each relevant ExpenseBot Linear issue using linear_get_issue to check current state, assignee, description, and acceptance criteria. Discover that ENG-214 and ENG-215 are still marked in_progress despite Lena confirming both config fixes deployed and verified in production. Discover that ENG-217 has acceptance criteria with checkboxes still unchecked including the schema validation gate item.
OE 5: Read comments on key ExpenseBot Linear tickets using linear_list_comments. On the pipeline hardening ticket (ENG-217, issue_pipeline_hardening), find Dmitri's audit results comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results) posted as context for hardening work - it lists all 5 Vectral false rejections and 3 Mosaic false approvals with per-record details, the $885 financial risk, and notes James Okafor's categorization issue. On the edge cases ticket (ENG-212, issue_edge_cases_backlog), find Dmitri's validation tests comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests) listing 6 schema validation tests implemented in the ENG-212 branch: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, and test_no_silent_fallback - all six pass with corrected configs but fail with originals. Dmitri notes that Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate. On the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211, issue_pilot_monitoring), find Samira's pause confirmation comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed) documenting the remediation plan, task assignments, and timeline with earliest re-enablement week of 3/30. On the Mosaic config fix ticket (ENG-215, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), find Lena's deployment verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming config corrections deployed and verified in production for both Vectral and Mosaic, with test results for specific expenses, and noting she is closing ENG-214 and moving to pipeline hardening (ENG-217).
OE 6: Get details on the launch readiness checklist ticket (ENG-219, assigned to Samira) using linear_get_issue. Find the proposed launch gate criteria that must be satisfied before re-enabling auto-decisions: all validation tests passing, configuration data checked against source, and AM approval.
OE 7: Search Slack channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history with queries related to "launch" or "readiness" or "relaunch" or "ExpenseBot" or "validation gate" (or similar). Find Samira's remediation strategy message listing the timeline and task assignments (disable auto-decisions, full audit, pipeline fixes, dashboard type validation, launch readiness checklist), Anh's dashboard progress update confirming staging deployment, Lena's update on the core engine completion, and Dmitri's duplicate receipt discovery post. Also find Lena's message about incorporating Dmitri's schema tests into the pipeline deployment gate in ENG-217 - stated in future tense ("I will incorporate them"), indicating the integration was planned but not yet confirmed complete. The agent should note that ENG-217 remains in_progress and reconcile Lena's future-tense plans against the ticket's current state.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "audit" or "ExpenseBot" or "stipend" or "pilot" (or similar). Find communications about Dmitri's audit results (47 expenses total, 39 correct at 83% accuracy, 5 false rejections for Vectral, 3 false approvals for Mosaic), Samira's formal pilot pause directive, Elena's directive mandating the full audit, and the remediation plan.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for records related to stipend holds or suspicious reimbursements using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices (or similar). Check whether Marcus actually placed financial holds on the flagged stipend transactions. Compare what QuickBooks reflects against what the Airtable Stipend Transactions table shows to identify any gaps between the finance and engineering data views.
OE 10: Search Linear for the invoice-related safety ticket using linear_list_issues with query "invoice" or "attachment" or "email template" (or similar). Find the ticket titled "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches" (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, ENG-187, state: backlog, P2, assigned to Lena). Discover it was originally created as a follow-up safeguard after the Terraform pricing leak incident and has been sitting in backlog.
OE 11: Search Slack and emails for context on the client billing incident that made the invoice safety ticket relevant again. Use conversations_search_messages with queries like "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "attachment" (or similar) and search_emails with similar queries. Discover that Hana Kim accidentally swapped invoice PDF attachments between Sunbelt Venture Partners ($4,800 standard rate) and Palmetto Community Foundation ($2,400 nonprofit rate), exposing the nonprofit pricing to a for-profit client. This is the "client billing incident" that Samira referenced. Also find the related ops Linear ticket (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure") which tracks the operational response to the incident.
OE 12: Update incorrect Airtable Stipend Transaction records using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). For Mosaic false approvals that still incorrectly show "Approved": Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021), Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership (recStip000000022), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes (recStip000000023) should be changed from "Approved" to "Pending" or similar since they were auto-approved by the broken ExpenseBot config. For Vectral false rejections that were never manually corrected: Maria Santos's $550 standing desk (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, currently "Pending Approval"), James Okafor's $82 keyboard tray (recStip000000019, currently "Rejected"), and Lisa Park's $130 WiFi system (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi, currently "Pending Approval") should be changed to "Approved" since these are legitimate expenses within Vectral's policy limits. Additionally, Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) should be updated from "Approved" to "Pending" or similar since it is flagged as a duplicate receipt pending HR verification.
OE 13: Update the state of completed ExpenseBot Linear tickets using linear_update_issue. Move ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) to done since Lena confirmed both fixes deployed and verified in production. Leave ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) in its current state since the schema validation gate integration is not confirmed complete. Update any other tickets where the current state does not reflect actual status.
OE 14: Add comments to relevant Linear tickets using linear_create_comment. Add a comment on ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, issue_pipeline_hardening) documenting what is confirmed complete versus what remains open — specifically noting that Lena's config corrections for both Vectral and Mosaic are deployed and verified (per comment_lena_deploy_verified), Dmitri's 6 schema validation tests pass with corrected configs, but the integration of those tests into the deployment pipeline as a blocking gate is not confirmed complete (Lena's Slack message used future tense: "I will incorporate them"). Add a comment on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (invoice safety ticket, ENG-187) noting the direct connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident and recommending priority reassessment given that the exact class of risk this ticket was designed to prevent has now materialized.
OE 15: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: team_engineering) for tracking the Airtable data remediation effort if no existing ticket covers it. Title should reference ExpenseBot data remediation or stipend record cleanup (or similar). Description should document: the 3 Mosaic false approvals changed from "Approved" to "Pending" (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023), the 3 Vectral false rejections corrected to "Approved" (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, recStip000000019, rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi), Kevin Tran's duplicate-flagged keyboard changed to "Pending" pending HR verification, and any remaining items requiring AM sign-off before final disposition.
OE 16: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira" or "Samira Tariq" (or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 17: Look up Dmitri Volkov's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Dmitri" or "Dmitri Volkov" (or similar). Find dmitri.volkov@moveops.com.
OE 18: Send an email using send_email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com covering: which ExpenseBot tickets have been confirmed done and closed (ENG-214, ENG-215), which are still open and blocking relaunch (ENG-217 with the schema validation gate integration still unconfirmed based on Lena's future-tense Slack message, ENG-219 launch readiness criteria not yet met), the data remediation actions taken in Airtable stipend records (Mosaic false approvals changed from "Approved" to "Pending," Vectral false rejections corrected to "Approved" for the 3 uncorrected records, duplicate-flagged Kevin Tran keyboard changed to "Pending"), the invoice safety ticket (ENG-187) and its direct connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident, and recommendations for what needs to happen before relaunch.
OE 19: Post a status update in the #engineering channel (channel C003) using conversations_add_message summarizing the current state of ExpenseBot relaunch readiness: which tickets are closed (ENG-214, ENG-215), which are still blocking (ENG-217 schema gate pending, ENG-219 launch criteria not met), the data remediation actions performed in Airtable stipend records (Mosaic false approvals flagged, Vectral false rejections corrected, duplicate receipt flagged), and status against the launch readiness gates that Samira defined.
OE 20: Schedule a 30-minute calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for early next week (Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026) with Dmitri Volkov (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) as an attendee, titled something like "ExpenseBot Data Remediation Sync" (or similar), to align on the data remediation plan before the relaunch conversation happens.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search or list Airtable Stipend Transactions records for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table: Stipend Transactions, field: Account, value: "Mosaic Robotics"). All three still show "Approved" despite being identified as erroneous during the pilot audit. Also find Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Approval Status: Pending Approval, flagged by Dmitri with a duplicate SHA-256 hash matching Kevin Tran's submission).

OE 13: Update the state of completed ExpenseBot Linear tickets using linear_update_issue. Move ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) to done since Lena confirmed both fixes deployed and verified in production. Leave ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) in its current state since the schema validation gate integration is not confirmed complete. Update any other tickets where the current state does not reflect actual status.
OE 14: Add comments to relevant Linear tickets using linear_create_comment. Add a comment on ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, issue_pipeline_hardening) documenting what is confirmed complete versus what remains open — specifically noting that Lena's config corrections for both Vectral and Mosaic are deployed and verified (per comment_lena_deploy_verified), Dmitri's 6 schema validation tests pass with corrected configs, but the integration of those tests into the deployment pipeline as a blocking gate is not confirmed complete (Lena's Slack message used future tense: "I will incorporate them"). Add a comment on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (invoice safety ticket, ENG-187) noting the direct connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident and recommending priority reassessment given that the exact class of risk this ticket was designed to prevent has now materialized.
OE 15: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: team_engineering) for tracking the Airtable data remediation effort if no existing ticket covers it. Title should reference ExpenseBot data remediation or stipend record cleanup (or similar). Description should document: the 3 Mosaic false approvals changed from "Approved" to "Pending" (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023), the 3 Vectral false rejections corrected to "Approved" (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, recStip000000019, rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi), Kevin Tran's duplicate-flagged keyboard changed to "Pending" pending HR verification, and any remaining items requiring AM sign-off before final disposition.
OE 16: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira" or "Samira Tariq" (or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 17: Look up Dmitri Volkov's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Dmitri" or "Dmitri Volkov" (or similar). Find dmitri.volkov@moveops.com.
OE 18: Send an email using send_email from anh.nguyen@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com covering: which ExpenseBot tickets have been confirmed done and closed (ENG-214, ENG-215), which are still open and blocking relaunch (ENG-217 with the schema validation gate integration still unconfirmed based on Lena's future-tense Slack message, ENG-219 launch readiness criteria not yet met), the data remediation actions taken in Airtable stipend records (Mosaic false approvals changed from "Approved" to "Pending," Vectral false rejections corrected to "Approved" for the 3 uncorrected records, duplicate-flagged Kevin Tran keyboard changed to "Pending"), the invoice safety ticket (ENG-187) and its direct connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident, and recommendations for what needs to happen before relaunch.
OE 19: Post a status update in the #engineering channel (channel C003) using conversations_add_message summarizing the current state of ExpenseBot relaunch readiness: which tickets are closed (ENG-214, ENG-215), which are still blocking (ENG-217 schema gate pending, ENG-219 launch criteria not met), the data remediation actions performed in Airtable stipend records (Mosaic false approvals flagged, Vectral false rejections corrected, duplicate receipt flagged), and status against the launch readiness gates that Samira defined.
OE 20: Schedule a 30-minute calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for early next week (Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28, 2026) with Dmitri Volkov (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) as an attendee, titled something like "ExpenseBot Data Remediation Sync" (or similar), to align on the data remediation plan before the relaunch conversation happens.
